title: Consumer-package test bypasses npm pack, so publish-time packaging regressions can still pass
reasoning: The test comments claim it reproduces `npm pack → install into temp project → tsc`, but the implementation never runs `npm pack`. Instead it manually creates a fake `node_modules/@gitlawb/openclaude`, copies source files directly from the checkout, and writes dummy `dist/*.mjs` placeholders. That means a broken `prepack`, a missing file in the tarball, or a bad `files` whitelist can all slip through while this test still passes.
recommendation: Make the test consume a real packed tarball from `npm pack --json`, install that tarball into the temp project, and run `tsc` against the installed package contents.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: The current setup uses checkout files as a stand-in for the published package, so it never validates the actual package archive layout or publish-time file list.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add an assertion that `npm pack --json` includes the intended export files, then install the generated tarball into the temp consumer and compile against that installation.
